items.explain: By convention, 0 is success; non-zero is failure.

choices: The exit status of the previous command
items.explain: $? is the exit status of the last foreground command.

items.prompt: Ctrl+C in the terminal sends an interrupt signal to the foreground process.
items.explain: Ctrl+C delivers SIGINT to the foreground job.

choices: A polite terminate that processes can catch
A last-resort force kill that cannot be caught
items.explain: SIGKILL (-9) cannot be caught; use it only when gentler signals fail.
